behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names both files (client-fixture.mjs, bake-harness.ts), the function loadPage, the unhandledRejection handler requirement, exit code propagation to this.output.exitCode, and the content-type handling — all test assertions reference these.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes problems (no retry, null header TypeError, missing handler) without prescribing exact code changes like 'replace line X with Y'.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check behavior (retry count >=3, no crash on null content-type, exit code 34, proc.exited assignment to this.output.exitCode) which the instruction explicitly requires, and allow multiple implementations.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute node subprocesses, extract and run loadPage against a live HTTP server, trigger unhandled rejections, and check exit codes.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub loadPage would fail the null-ct test (requires req>=1 and no crash) and retry test (requires req>=3 with success); unhandled rejection test requires actual exit code 34.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All five instruction points (retry, null content-type, unhandledRejection, initial load try/catch implicitly, exit code propagation) are covered by tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with comment headers (Gate, Fail-to-pass) and descriptive names with docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code-fix, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names (client-fixture.mjs, bake-harness.ts, exitCodeMap, reloadFailed) are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction references 'around line 817' which is a guidance hint but does not dictate the exact code; files are already named in the problem domain.
